title: Meridian US Municipal Bond Fund
content:
MERIDIAN US MUNICIPAL BOND FUND - Product Factsheet

Fund Objective: The Meridian US Municipal Bond Fund seeks to provide current income exempt from federal income tax while preserving capital through investments in high-quality municipal securities. The fund maintains a diversified portfolio of investment-grade municipal bonds with an average duration of 4.2 years and weighted average maturity of 6.8 years.

Portfolio Composition: As of the last reporting period, the fund holds 247 individual municipal bond positions across 38 states and territories. Geographic allocation includes California (18.3%), New York (14.7%), Texas (12.1%), Florida (8.9%), and Illinois (7.2%), with the remainder distributed across other states. The fund maintains sector diversification with general obligation bonds comprising 42.1% of holdings, revenue bonds at 38.6%, essential service bonds at 12.8%, and other municipal securities at 6.5%.

Credit Quality: Consistent with our conservative risk approach, 89.3% of holdings maintain investment-grade ratings. The credit quality breakdown shows AAA-rated securities at 31.2%, AA-rated at 28.7%, A-rated at 19.8%, BBB-rated at 9.6%, with 10.7% in unrated securities that have been internally evaluated and deemed investment-grade equivalent by our credit research team.

Performance Metrics: The fund's 30-day SEC yield stands at 3.68% as of the most recent calculation, with a tax-equivalent yield of 5.12% for investors in the highest federal tax bracket. Over the trailing twelve months, the fund has delivered a total return of 4.23%, outperforming the Bloomberg Municipal Bond Index by 47 basis points. Duration risk is actively managed with current modified duration of 4.18 years.

Fee Structure: The fund operates with a competitive expense ratio of 0.58% annually. For client accounts exceeding $1 million, a reduced expense ratio of 0.48% applies. Transaction costs average 0.12% due to our institutional trading relationships and efficient portfolio management practices.

Regulatory Considerations: In accordance with MSRB Rule G-19, all municipal securities transactions are executed with appropriate price transparency and best execution standards. Our recommendation process adheres to FINRA Rule 2111 suitability requirements, ensuring this fund aligns with clients' investment objectives, financial circumstances, and risk tolerance. This product is generally suitable for investors seeking tax-advantaged income, capital preservation, and portfolio diversification.

Risk Factors: Primary risks include interest rate sensitivity, credit risk of underlying municipal issuers, and potential changes in tax legislation affecting municipal bond tax advantages. The fund may experience principal fluctuation, particularly in rising interest rate environments. State-specific concentration risk exists, though geographic diversification mitigates this exposure.

Liquidity: The fund provides daily liquidity with settlement occurring T+1 for redemptions. During periods of market stress, liquidity may be reduced, and the fund maintains a 2% cash position plus additional liquid securities to meet redemption demands.

Suitability Profile: This fund is appropriate for conservative investors seeking tax-advantaged income, particularly those in higher tax brackets. Typical client profiles include retirees, high-net-worth individuals seeking portfolio diversification, and investors with intermediate-term investment horizons of 3-7 years. The fund complements equity allocations and provides stability during market volatility while generating tax-efficient income.
